action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent syntax error in test_preprocess.py (bad lambda comprehension) + truncated predict.py + ObservationProcessorStep error (language step mutates/drops 'observation' key). All evals 0.0 from fallback actions; mock sometimes reaches success but real rollouts fail. PI0 loads and RecordingEvaluator structure are solid per history. Analysis: Training pipeline requires careful step-by-step application with deepcopy+language merge after LanguageProcessorStep to preserve observation (per 10+ iters of diagnostics). Complete non-truncated predict.py with _safe_preprocess (sequential processors + explicit language merge before observation steps, PIL 224 imgs, padded 32D state/action, dummy action, combined batch), full predict() with action chunking+pending queue, tuned ee-delta bridge (pos*0.05, euler*0.25, grip>0.5->1/-1), complete main() parsing eval_config.json+track_path, and RecordingEvaluator (proper super().__init__ + evaluate_single_episode calling super per contract for exact JSON episodes list) will fix root cause. This stays on intended Pi0 track (LIBERO-pretrained should give >0 success on add_condiment/select once adapter works). Decision: Overwrite test_preprocess.py (clean sequential diagnostic with per-step prints+merge, no syntax errors) and predict.py (FULL complete code based on partial in workspace + working _safe_preprocess from diagnostic pattern). Run python test_preprocess.py && python test_obs.py for validation (expect FULL PREPROCESS SUCCEEDED + real policy actions in mock without traceback). Then submit_eval on base checkpoint for first non-zero success_rate before adding LoRA/training.
